extraction.one_line_summary: CARUSO Damiano timed his solo breakaway perfectly, holding off ROGLIC Primoz by 1:05 on the Velefique MTF despite minimal gap entering the climb.
tactical_patterns: Ineos replaced Jumbo-Visma pacemaking on lower Velefique slopes, signaling they rejected Jumbo's deliberately soft tempo favoring breakaway survival.
CARUSO's gap reached 5:15 mid-stage but only 1.5min on GC-equivalent time, indicating Jumbo calculated exact threshold for stage win.
ROGLIC sprinted for time bonuses at MTF despite stage loss, prioritizing 6-second gains over chasing when outcome uncertain.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: identified as having 'least to lose' suggesting Ineos already writing off his GC, focusing aggression elsewhere
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: cracked badly losing 1:11 to Roglic on Velefique, forced Ineos to 'go back to drawing board' mid-race
rider_signals.HAGUE Jack: positive: unexpected best-of-rest ride for Bahrain after Landa struggles, emerged as alternative protected rider
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: held 1:05 advantage despite only 1.5min GC-equivalent buffer entering final climb, perfect energy management from domestique role
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: sprinted for time bonuses even after stage loss sealed, maximizing every marginal gain opportunity
